slots.notes: Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Try to keep the cable and your wrist aligned in a straight line throughout the pull. Feel a nice, deep lat stretch at the top.
Pec Static Stretch (30 sec hold) Set the bench at a ~15° incline. 1 second pause on the chest on each rep while maintaining tension on the pecs.
N/A Mind-muscle connection with your inner thighs. These are great for adding thigh mass from the front! Push them hard!

Quad Static Stretch (30 sec hold) Feet lower on the platform for more quad focus. Get as deep as you can without excessive back rounding. Control the negative and do a slight pause at the bottom of each rep. Try to add a little weight each week at the same rep count.
N/A Pause for 1-2 seconds in the squeeze of each rep. Contract the rear delts hard!
N/A Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.
N/A Slightly rotate the dumbbells in on the negative and flare your elbows out as you push.

N/A The RPE is intentionally low here because these will cause a lot of muscle damage. Don't be tempted to go too heavy. 1 second pause at the bottom of each rep. To keep tension on the hamstrings, stop about 75% of the way to full lockout on each rep (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the range of motion).
Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Flare elbows out at roughly 45° and squeeze your shoulder blades together hard at the top of each rep.
N/A These will target the brachialis and forearms hard. Squeeze the dumbbell hard in the middle of the handle and curl about 3/4 of the way up (i.e. stay in the bottom 3/4 of the curl).
N/A Raise the cables up and out in a "Y" motion. Really try to connect with the middle delt fibers as you sweep the weight up and out.
N/A Feel a nasty stretch on the triceps throughout the entire negative. Pause for 1 second in the stretch part of each rep.

Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Slow 2-3 second negative. Feel your lats pulling apart on the way down. Slight 0.5-1 second pause at the bottom, then lift your chest up and drive your elbows down as you lift yourself up. Don't be afraid to use assistance. Keep the form tight and controlled!
N/A Slow 2-3 second negative. 1-2 second pause at the bottom. Explode with control on the way up. Go as deep as your shoulders comfortably allow, trying to at least break a 90° elbow angle.
Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Lean forward over the machine to get a maximum stretch in your hamstrings.

Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Set the seat back as far as it will go while still feeling comfortable. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat. Use a 2-3 second negative. Feel your quads pulling apart on the negative.
N/A Shrug up and in. Think about shrugging "up to your ears". 1-2 second pause in the squeeze (at the top) of each rep, then another 1-2 second pause in the stretch (at the bottom) of each rep.
N/A Allow your lower back to round as you curl your legs up. 10-20 reps is a broad range on purpose: just go until you hit RPE 9-10 (0-1 reps shy of failure) with controlled form.

N/A Because we did seated hamstring work yesterday, these are mainly meant to get the knees nice and warmed up before hack squats. Don't push these too hard today, just feel the hamstrings moving the weight under control.

N/A We're using a reverse pyramid on this exercise. Warm-up as usual to your first working set for 4 reps. This first set will be your heaviest set. Then for set 2, drop the weight back ~10-15% and do 6 reps. Then for set 3, drop the weight back another 10-15% and do 8 reps.
N/A Lean forward until your torso is parallel with the floor, flye straight out and down toward the floor. Stretch and squeeze the pecs! Stay locked in.

Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Do these pulldowns with the handle more out in front of you, more like a cross between pullover and a pulldown. Focus on feeling your lats working more than the weight you're using.
Calf Static Stretch (30 sec) 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

Mechanical Dropset (on all sets) You'll probably want to watch the video for this one. Take ~3 big steps back from the cable machine and do your first 5 reps. After those first 5 reps, immediately (without resting) take 1 step forward and do another 4 reps. Then (without resting) take another step forward and do at least another 3 reps (or until you hit RPE 9-10).
N/A Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.

N/A If your weak point is feeling recovered (not sore or fatigued) then feel free to hit Exercise 2. If your weak point is feeling tired or sore, do not perform the second weak point exercise this week.

N/A If you have a left-right bicep size imbalance, do these 1 arm at a time, starting with the weaker arm. Take the weaker arm to an RPE of 9-10. Then match the reps with the other arm (stop once you've matched the reps, even if the RPE is lower). If you don't have a size imbalance, do these both arms at the same time.

Dropset These are meant to be fairly heavy, which is why we're using a bar instead of a rope. Aim to add some weight week to week at the set rep target. Always keep the form tight as you overload the triceps.

N/A Stay in the bottom 2/3 of the curl. Don't squeeze all the way up to the top. Keep your triceps firmly pinned against the pad as you curl. No pausing at the top or bottom: constant tension on the biceps!

N/A There are two ways you can do this: upright or bent over. Choose the one that feels more comfortable for you. The main thing is that when you're in the full squeeze, your shoulder should be positioned back behind your torso.
Calf Static Stretch (30 sec) 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.
Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Try to keep the cable and your wrist aligned in a straight line throughout the pull. Feel a nice, deep lat stretch at the top.

Calf Static Stretch (30 sec) 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Keep your torso locked in a fixed position (don't lean forward on the negative). Drive your elbows down and back to engage the lats. Keep your elbows tucked in to your sides.

N/A Set the bench at a ~15° incline. Do a slight elbow tuck on the negative and then flare as you push (assuming this doesn't bother your shoulders). Nice, smooth reps here. No pausing at the top or bottom: constant tension on the pecs!

Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Set the machine so that you get the biggest stretch possible at the bottom. Prevent your butt from popping up as you curl. Once you can't get to the full squeeze, continue with partial reps on the last set.

N/A We're using a reverse pyramid on this exercise. Warm-up as usual to your first working set for 4 reps. This first set will be your heaviest set. Then for set 2, drop the weight back ~10-15% and do 6 reps. Then for set 3, drop the weight back another 10-15% and do 8 reps.

Integrated Partials (on all sets) Sweep the weight out instead of pulling the weight back. Mind-muscle connection with rear delts. On all sets, alternate full-ROM reps and half-ROM reps (i.e. do 1 rep with full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM (in the stretched/bottom half), then 1 rep full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM). Repeat until you've reached the target reps and an RPE of 9-10.
Myo-reps Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.
Myo-reps Raise the cables up in a "Y" motion. Really try to connect with the middle delt fibers as you sweep the weight up and out.

N/A Do 8-10 reps as a normal T-Bar row, driving your elbows back at roughly 45° and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Without resting, do another 4-6 reps as Kelso Shrugs (just squeeze your shoulder blades together without rowing all the way back with your arms).
Dropset Ensure that your elbows break at least 90°. Mind-muscle connection with your delts. Smooth, controlled reps.
Calf Static Stretch (30 sec) 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

N/A The RPE is intentionally low here because these will cause a lot of muscle damage. Don't be tempted to go too heavy. Lower the dumbbells with a 3-4 second negative. If you want to target more glutes, squeeze them hard at the top as you get to full lockout. If you want to target more hamstrings, only go to 3/4 of full lockout and maintain constant tension on your hamstrings.

N/A Do concentration curls on one arm, rest ~0.5-1 min, do triceps extensions for the same arm, rest ~0.5-1 min, do concentration curls on the other arm, rest ~0.5-1 min, do triceps extensions on the other arm, rest ~0.5-1 min, repeat.
Dropset
Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Lean back by ~15° and drive your elbows down as you squeeze your shoulder blades together. This should feel like a mix of lats and mid-traps.
N/A Feel your pecs stretching apart on the negative. Mind-muscle connection with lower pecs.
N/A If possible, use pads to increase the range of motion on the machine. Lean forward and grab onto the machine rails to stretch the glutes further.
N/A Mind-muscle connection with your inner thighs. These are great for adding thigh mass from the front! Push them hard!

Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Set the seat back as far as it will go while still feeling comfortable. Grab the handles as hard as you can to pull your butt down into the seat. Use a 2-3 second negative. Feel your quads pulling apart on the negative.

N/A Perform lateral raises as normal, except going until your hands are up overhead. As you break parallel, you will use more upper traps to move the weight. Feel free to squeeze your upper traps at the top. If you feel shoulder pain when going all the way up, try pointing your thumb up or simply stop at parallel and do normal lateral raises.
N/A Keep the ball held out far from your body on the sides and control the reps, don't just rush through them to get the set done.
N/A Lean forward over the machine to get a maximum stretch in your hamstrings.
N/A Set one leg back on the negative and then drive the weight up using your front leg. Try to minimize assistance from your back leg.

Integrated Partials (on all sets) Set up the pec deck to allow for maximum stretch. On all sets, alternate full-ROM reps and half-ROM reps (i.e. do 1 rep with full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM (in the stretched/bottom half), then 1 rep full-ROM, then 1 rep half-ROM). Repeat until you've reached the target reps and an RPE of 9-10.

Dropset Set up a wide grip pulldown bar on a seated cable row. Using a double overhand grip, perform rows while leaning forward on the negative and then extend your torso to be upright as you finish the row.

N/A Hold a dumbbell and perform a jumping motion without actually leaving the floor, using a slight knee bend, but mostly relying on your calves/ankles to drive the "jump". I believe I built a lot of calf mass by doing jump rope; these are meant to provide a similar stimulus, but with more tension.
N/A Pull with one arm at a time, bracing with your non-working hand against the machine. Try to align your arm and the cable in a straight line at the bottom of the flye.
N/A Decide on your weak point using The Weak Point Table in your Hypertrophy Handbook. Perform ONE of the exercises listed under Exercise 1 for the sets and reps provided here.

N/A If your weak point is feeling recovered (not sore or fatigued) then feel free to hit Exercise 2. If your weak point is feeling tired or sore, do not perform the second weak point exercise this week.

N/A Use a 3-4 second negative. Arc the EZ-bar slightly back behind your head. When you extend, keep the bar back behind your eye line. Use the inside (closer) grip option and allow the elbows to flare to a degree that feels comfortable.
Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Use a 3-4 second negative and a slight pause at the bottom of each rep to emphasize stretching your biceps.

N/A Use two long ropes or one long rope. Lean slightly forward, flare your elbows slightly out and keep your arms back in line with your torso. Then do triceps pressdowns, getting a full, big squeeze at the bottom.

N/A Squeeze the dumbbell hard in the middle of the handle as you curl. Using liquid chalk on these will prevent your grip from slipping, keeping your hand in the middle of the handle throughout the set (as opposed to resting against the head of the dumbbell).

Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Keep your torso locked in a fixed position (don't lean forward on the negative). Drive your elbows down and back to engage the lats. Keep your elbows tucked in to your sides.

N/A Set the bench at a ~15° incline. Do a slight elbow tuck on the negative and then flare as you push (assuming this doesn't bother your shoulders). Nice, smooth reps here. No pausing at the top or bottom: constant tension on the pecs!

N/A Use a 3-4 second negative. Arc the EZ-bar slightly back behind your head. When you extend, keep the bar back behind your eye line. Use the inside (closer) grip option and allow the elbows to flare to a degree that feels comfortable.
Long-length Partials (on all reps of the last set) Use a 3-4 second negative and a slight pause at the bottom of each rep to emphasize stretching your biceps.

N/A Use two long ropes or one long rope. Lean slightly forward, flare your elbows slightly out and keep your arms back in line with your torso. Then do triceps pressdowns, getting a full, big squeeze at the bottom.

N/A Squeeze the dumbbell hard in the middle of the handle as you curl. Using liquid chalk on these will prevent your grip from slipping, keeping your hand in the middle of the handle throughout the set (as opposed to resting against the head of the dumbbell).
